# services.cjdns

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.cjdns`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.beacon` | `signed integer` | Auto-connect to other cjdns nodes on the same network. Options: 0: Disabled. 1: Accept beacons, this will cause cjdns to accept incoming beacon messages and try connecting to the sender. 2: Accept and send beacons, this will cause cjdns to broadcast messages on the local network which contain a randomly generated per-session password, other nodes which have this set to 1 or 2 will hear the beacon messages and connect automatically. |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.bind` | `string` | Bind to this device for native ethernet operation. `all` is a pseudo-name which will try to connect to all devices. |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.connectTo` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Credentials for connecting look similar to UDP credientials except they begin with the mac address. |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.connectTo.<name>.hostname` | `string` | Optional hostname to add to /etc/hosts; prevents reverse lookup failures. |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.connectTo.<name>.login` | `string` | (optional) name your peer has for you |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.connectTo.<name>.password` | `string` | Authorized password to the opposite end of the tunnel. |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.connectTo.<name>.peerName` | `string` | (optional) human-readable name for peer |
| `services.cjdns.ETHInterface.connectTo.<name>.publicKey` | `string` | Public key at the opposite end of the tunnel. |
| `services.cjdns.UDPInterface.bind` | `string` | Address and port to bind UDP tunnels to. |
| `services.cjdns.UDPInterface.connectTo` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Credentials for making UDP tunnels. |
| `services.cjdns.UDPInterface.connectTo.<name>.hostname` | `string` | Optional hostname to add to /etc/hosts; prevents reverse lookup failures. |
| `services.cjdns.UDPInterface.connectTo.<name>.login` | `string` | (optional) name your peer has for you |
| `services.cjdns.UDPInterface.connectTo.<name>.password` | `string` | Authorized password to the opposite end of the tunnel. |
| `services.cjdns.UDPInterface.connectTo.<name>.peerName` | `string` | (optional) human-readable name for peer |
| `services.cjdns.UDPInterface.connectTo.<name>.publicKey` | `string` | Public key at the opposite end of the tunnel. |
| `services.cjdns.addExtraHosts` | `boolean` | Whether to add cjdns peers with an associated hostname to {file}`/etc/hosts`. Beware that enabling this incurs heavy eval-time costs. |
| `services.cjdns.admin.bind` | `string` | Bind the administration port to this address and port. |
| `services.cjdns.authorizedPasswords` | `list of string` | Any remote cjdns nodes that offer these passwords on connection will be allowed to route through this node. |
| `services.cjdns.confFile` | `null or absolute path` | Ignore all other cjdns options and load configuration from this file. |
| `services.cjdns.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the cjdns network encryption and routing engine. A file at /etc/cjdns.keys will be created if it does not exist to contain a random secret key that your IPv6 address will be derived from. |
| `services.cjdns.extraConfig` | `attribute set` | Extra configuration, given as attrs, that will be merged recursively with the rest of the JSON generated by this module, at the root node. |
